steps.title: Track the interruption
steps.content: The base sentence is "Scott-Heron himself resisted the godfather nickname." The word "however" interrupts that clause, and the phrase beginning "feeling" also follows as supplementary information. The punctuation needs commas to set off the interrupter while keeping the sentence unified.

steps.title: Use matching punctuation
steps.content: A leaves "however" unclosed before the following phrase. B and D use semicolons where the sentence needs commas, incorrectly breaking the main clause from its supplement. The choice with commas after both "nickname" and "however" fits the structure. The answer is C.
